originalTitle: Fifa to pay Somali referee Artan full World Cup fee
title: Our African Referee Hero Gets Paid!
content:
Hello, my super friends! I have a really happy story from our amazing continent, Africa! It's about a very special grown-up named Omar Artan. He is from Somalia, which is a country in Africa, just like many of our countries! Omar is a referee, and he loves football just like many of you do! Guess what? Omar Artan was chosen to be a referee at the biggest football party in the whole world, the World Cup! Wow! But then, a little problem happened, and he couldn't go. But don't worry, there's a happy ending! Let's find out all about it.

Meet Our Football Friend, Omar Artan!

Omar Artan is a referee. Do you know what a referee does in a football game? Well, a referee is like the big boss on the field! They wear a special uniform, often black, and they have a whistle. Their job is super important! They make sure all the players follow the rules of the game. If someone breaks a rule, the referee blows their whistle and makes a decision. They make sure the game is fair and fun for everyone. Omar is a very good referee, one of the best! *Did you know?* The referee is sometimes called the "man in black" because their uniform is often black! But some referees wear other colours too, like yellow or green! Omar is from Somalia, a beautiful country in East Africa. He has worked very, very hard to become such a good referee. He has to study many rules and practice making quick decisions. He has to be very brave and fair, just like a leader on the field. Being a referee for big games means you are truly special.

The Biggest Football Party: The World Cup!

Imagine the biggest, most exciting football party in the whole world! That's what the World Cup is! It happens only every four years. Teams from many different countries, all the way from Africa, Europe, Asia, and everywhere else, come together to play football. They all want to win the big golden trophy! Being part of the World Cup is a very, very big deal. It means you are one of the best at what you do, whether you are a player, a coach, or a referee. It's a dream come true for many people who love football. Omar Artan worked so hard to get this big chance! *Did you know?* Africa has sent many amazing teams to the World Cup! Like Ghana, Nigeria, Senegal, Cameroon, and South Africa! They make our continent proud with their fantastic football skills and loud cheering fans!

Omar's Big Chance and a Little Problem

Omar Artan was chosen to be a referee at the World Cup! This was going to be in a country called the United States. It's very far away from Somalia, across a big ocean. Omar was so excited! He trained hard and got ready for this big adventure. He was ready to pack his bags and travel far to do his important job. But then, something a little sad happened. Omar couldn't go to the United States. It was like he had his bags packed, and his ticket ready, but the door just wouldn't open for him to travel there. This meant he couldn't go to the World Cup to do his important referee job, even though he was ready and chosen. Imagine how sad that would make someone feel, right? It was a big disappointment because he had worked so hard and was really looking forward to it. He missed his chance to stand on the big World Cup field and make sure the game was fair. But this is not the end of his story!

Good News from FIFA!

Even though Omar couldn't go, there's a big grown-up group called FIFA. FIFA is like the head teacher for all the football in the world! They make sure everything runs smoothly and fairly. They organize big tournaments like the World Cup. FIFA heard about Omar's problem. And guess what? They decided to do something super fair and kind! FIFA said, "Even though Omar couldn't go to the World Cup, he worked very hard to get ready. He was chosen because he is a fantastic referee. So, we will still pay him all the money he was supposed to get for his job!" This money is called a "tournament fee." It's like the money grown-ups get for doing a special job, like your mum or dad gets paid for their work. It's for all his hard work and preparation. *Did you know?* FIFA is a French name, but everyone around the world calls it FIFA! It stands for Fédération Internationale de Football Association. Big words for a big football group that takes care of football everywhere!

Why This is Super Fair!

Isn't that wonderful? Even though Omar couldn't travel, he still gets his full payment. Why is this so fair? Because he trained hard, he was ready, and it wasn't his fault he couldn't go. He did all his part, and he deserved to be paid for his effort and for being chosen as one of the best referees in the world. It shows that hard work is always important, and sometimes, even if things don't go exactly as planned, good things can still happen if people are fair. FIFA showed everyone that they care about their referees and want to do the right thing. This news means a lot, especially for all the young boys and girls in Africa who dream of playing or being part of big football events one day. It shows that people from Africa are important and their hard work is valued. It tells us that fairness is a very big deal in the world of sports.

What We Can Learn From Omar

Omar Artan's story teaches us a lot. It teaches us about being a good sport, even when things are tough. It teaches us about working hard to achieve our dreams. And it teaches us that fairness is very important in the world. When you play football with your friends, always try to be fair, just like a good referee! His story also reminds us that people from Africa are doing amazing things all over the world, even if we don't always hear about them. Omar is a hero, not just because he's a great referee, but because he shows us the spirit of determination and fairness. He is a shining example for all of us in Africa. *Did you know?* There are many amazing African referees who have officiated in big matches around the world! They are very good at making sure the game is fair and that everyone has fun playing or watching.

A Happy Ending!

So, even though Omar didn't get to blow his whistle on the big World Cup field, he still gets his full payment because he was ready and chosen for the job. It’s a happy ending that shows us the power of fairness and hard work. Let's clap for Omar Artan, our wonderful African referee from Somalia! Keep shining, Omar! And keep dreaming big, my little champions! You never know, one day you might be a referee or a player at the World Cup too!
summary: Somali referee Omar Artan, who couldn't travel to the World Cup, will still get his full payment from FIFA because he worked hard and was chosen for the big job.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that hard work is rewarded and that fairness matters, even if things don't go as planned, especially for our African friends doing great things around the world.

originalTitle: Sexual Violence in Israeli Prisons: What History Tells Us
title: Always Be Kind and Keep Everyone Safe!
content:
Hello, Super Friends, from Africa!

Hello, hello, my super friends! It's your friendly reporter, Zola, from sunny, beautiful Africa! I hope you are all having a wonderful day filled with laughter and sunshine. Today, we are going to talk about something super, super important. It’s about how we treat everyone and make sure everyone feels safe and happy, all the time. This is a very big job for grown-ups, and we can all help too!

Being Safe is a Super Power!

Imagine you are playing with your friends. You want everyone to feel safe, right? You want to know that no one will push you, or say unkind words. Feeling safe is like having a warm, cozy blanket around you. It means you are protected and no bad things should happen to you. It means you can play and learn and grow without being scared. Guess what? Every single person in the whole wide world deserves to feel safe. It doesn't matter where they come from, or what they look like, or what games they like to play. Everyone deserves to be safe and happy. This is a very important rule for all of us. Did you know? Feeling safe helps you learn better and makes you smile more!

Rules Help Us All

We all have rules, don't we? At home, at school, and even when you play outside. Rules are like special instructions that help us know what to do so everyone can get along and be safe. For example, at school, the rule might be 'no running in the hallway.' This rule helps keep everyone from falling down and getting hurt. Just like we have rules for playing and learning, grown-ups also have very important rules for how to treat other grown-ups. Especially when some grown-ups are in special places, like big buildings where they might need to stay for a while. These places also have very strict rules. These rules are there to make sure everyone is always treated with kindness and respect. It means no one should ever be made to feel scared or sad. No one should ever be treated badly. Grown-ups who are in charge of these special places have a big job to follow these rules very carefully. They must make sure everyone is always safe.

Learning from Yesterday

Have you ever heard stories from your grandparents or your teachers about things that happened a long, long time ago? We call these stories 'history.' History is like a big storybook about what happened in the past. We learn about history so we can understand things better today and make sure tomorrow is even better. Sometimes, in history, we learn that grown-ups didn't always follow the rules about being kind and keeping everyone safe. Sometimes, sad things happened that should never have happened. And sometimes, these sad things happened to people who were in those special big buildings. This is why it's so important to remember. We must learn from those times. We must learn that being kind and following the rules isn't just for *sometimes*. It has to be for *always*. And it has to be for *everyone*. Did you know? Some of the oldest stories in the world teach us about being fair and good to others!

Kindness Everywhere

Kindness is a magical thing! When you are kind to someone, it makes them feel good, and it makes you feel good too. Imagine if everyone, everywhere, always tried their very best to be kind. Imagine if all grown-ups always remembered to be kind to other grown-ups, no matter what. When we learn about history, it reminds us that we must work hard to make sure kindness and safety are everywhere. We want to make sure that in those special big buildings, and everywhere else, all grown-ups always treat each other with respect. This means using kind words, helping each other, and making sure no one feels scared or hurt. It’s like planting a little seed of kindness every day. If everyone plants a seed, soon we will have a whole garden of kindness!

Making Tomorrow Brighter

So, what does all this mean for us today? It means we need to remember that the rules about being kind and keeping everyone safe are super important. They are not just important when everyone is watching, or when something big happens on the news. They are important *all the time*. Grown-ups who work in places where they look after other grown-ups have a very special job. Their job is to make sure every single person is treated well, protected, and feels safe. They must remember the lessons from history, so those sad things never happen again. They must always follow the rules of kindness and safety. We want to build a world where everyone knows they will be safe, no matter what. A world where kindness shines bright like the African sun every single day. We learn from the past to make sure our future is full of smiles and safety for everyone. Did you know? When you help someone, your brain releases happy chemicals! It's true!

Your Part in Being Kind

Even though you are still small, you have a big heart! You can help make the world a kinder place. You can be kind to your friends, your family, and even people you don't know very well. You can share your toys, use your manners, and always try to make others feel good. And remember, if you ever see someone not being kind, or if you ever feel unsafe, it’s super important to tell a trusted grown-up. Like your teacher, or your mommy or daddy. They are there to help you and keep you safe. Together, by remembering to be kind and learning from yesterday, we can help all the grown-ups make sure everyone is always safe and treated fairly. Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends! Stay safe and keep shining brightly!
summary: This news item from Africa reminds us that everyone deserves to feel safe and be treated kindly, always, and that grown-ups must always follow rules to protect people, learning from past mistakes to make a better, safer future for all.
whyMatters: This is important because feeling safe and being treated kindly makes everyone happy and helps us all live better together, and we must always remember this for every person, everywhere, all the time.
